title: Comparative: President
lead: India's President: ==nominal executive==, indirectly elected with ==weighted, proportional voting==. The US President: ==real executive== with a direct popular mandate via the Electoral College.

head: Aspect
President of India
President of the US
rows: Indirect — ==proportional representation by single transferable vote==, secret ballot.
Indirect via popular vote + Electoral College; ==winner-takes-all== in most states.
Elected MPs + ==elected MLAs of states/UTs with legislatures== (nominated members excluded).
==Weighted==: MLA = (state population ÷ MLAs) × 1/1000; MP = total MLA value ÷ MPs.
One elector, one vote — no weighting.
Nationwide campaigns and debates are central.
==Arts 54–59== + Presidential Elections Act 1952; e.g. ==Droupadi Murmu, 15th President (2022)==.

blocks.text: India's election reflects ==federal balance and proportionality== through weighted legislator voting; the US system prioritises citizen participation — but can ==diverge from the popular vote== due to winner-takes-all.

head: Aspect
President of India
President of the US
rows: ==Nominal executive== (parliamentary system) — real power with PM & CoM.
==Real executive== — head of state AND government.
Citizen, 35+, LS-qualified, no office of profit.
On ==aid & advice of CoM (Art 74)==; appoints PM, Governors, judges; ceremonial ==Supreme Commander==.
Full executive power; appoints Cabinet/judges (Senate confirmation); ==real Commander-in-Chief==.
Summons/prorogues, dissolves LS, assent; ==suspensive veto== + unlimited ==pocket veto==; addresses joint sessions.
Signs/vetoes bills; ==pocket veto (10-day rule)==; no line-item veto; State of the Union.
Art 123 — when Parliament is not in session.
None — only Congress legislates; executive orders reviewable.
Broad — especially foreign policy and military.
==Direct popular mandate==; accountable to people and Congress.
